theme: aircraft taxiing across an active runway between gates
title: Wave Crossings on the Active Runway
seed_problem: route aircraft from gates across an active runway that opens only in periodic slots
mutation: composite: couple pushback scheduling (gate-alley blocking spaces same-alley pushbacks by d) with runway crossing that is legal only inside periodic width-W slots and one-lane (<=W aircraft per slot = a wave), scored by total weighted taxi delay; minimizing delay requires assembling full waves upstream, not routing aircraft one at a time.
innovation_hook: Slots reward BATCHED crossings, so the winning move is to shape pushback times upstream to assemble full waves at the crossing -- scheduling the queue, not the aircraft.
strategies: trivial: reproduce the judge baseline -- input order, push back ASAP, cross each aircraft alone in the next open slot (one per slot) -> ratio 0.1

greedy (the trap): FCFS -- push everyone back as early as release+alley allow in release order, then let the runway pass aircraft in arrival order into the earliest free slot tick; packs waves but ignores weight and never shapes the queue, so heavy aircraft spill to late waves

strong (insight): schedule the QUEUE into waves -- order pushbacks within each alley by weight (heavy first) so costly aircraft reach the crossing earliest, assign crossing ticks in weight order to pack each wave's W lanes, then pairwise-exchange ticks to polish weighted delay
beyond strong: joint search over pushback order and wave-to-slot assignment (local search / DP over the periodic slot structure) toward the weighted-completion-time optimum
